stimulus: The team that first cataloged the deep-sea snail Chrysomallon squamiferum chose a descriptive Latin name that highlighted its scaly iron foot. Most newly described species of deep-sea mollusks, however, receive only a numerical catalog entry, largely because the rate at which marine biologists now encounter new species during submersible surveys makes coining individual names ______blank.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. "Impractical" means not feasible. The text contrasts a single well-named species with the broader practice of assigning numerical entries, explaining that the high rate of new species discoveries makes individually naming each one unworkable.

Choice B is incorrect because the text discusses workload, not financial reward. Choice C is incorrect; if naming were routine, biologists would presumably name each species rather than assign numbers. Choice D is incorrect because the text does not suggest disagreement about naming, only that doing so is unmanageable at scale.
